decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to improve lateral heat distribution away from the chip hotspot. A conservative 20% increase is proposed, staying well within the allowed 2x step ratio and bounds.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity enhances lateral heat spreading, reducing the local thermal resistance under the chip and lowering the peak temperature.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate or boundary conditions rather than the spreader itself.
High conductivity materials may be cost-prohibitive in real-world applications, though this is a simulation study.
